ocr.text: Table 1: Body and heart weights and heart:body weight and RV:LV ratios in rat pups on postnatal day 14
Treatment n Body weight Heart weight Heart:body RV:LV
Values are expressed as the mean ± standard deviation.
*P < 0.05 vs. LPS+O2+NS group, **P < 0.01 vs. LPS+RA+NS group, and ***P < 0.001 vs. normal group.
Figure 1: (A) Representative lung sections stained with hematoxylin and eosin and (B) MWT in 14-day-old prenatal LPS-
and neonatal hyperoxia-treated rats and rats that received NS or human MSC treatment on postnatal day 5. Prenatal LPS and
neonatal hyperoxia treatment significantly increased the MWT in the rats on postnatal day 14. Compared with the NS-treated rats, the
MSC-treated rats exhibited a significantly decreased MWT on postnatal day 14 (*P < 0.05, **P < 0.01, and P < 0.001). Arrows indicate
